matches.match_rationale: The organization spends heavily on job boards and agencies partly because they lack compelling, tailored outreach materials and partnership proposals. AI text generation can produce role-specific job postings, referral program communications, and partnership pitch letters to teacher training colleges — content that currently requires manual effort or is simply absent. For niche roles like STEM and special education, well-crafted targeted outreach reduces time-to-fill without agency dependency.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-assisted content toolkit that auto-generates job postings, referral program invite emails, and partnership proposals to local teacher colleges, parameterized by role type and campus location.

matches.match_rationale: The organization has no structured way to surface past applicants, alumni, or internal candidates who could fill current openings — meaning every vacancy triggers a fresh expensive search. Semantic search over a candidate database (even a modest ATS export) allows recruiters to match job descriptions against prior applicants by skills and experience rather than exact keyword overlap, directly reducing agency dependency for repeat or similar roles.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A semantic candidate matching layer on top of the existing ATS or spreadsheet-based applicant archive, letting HR enter a role description and retrieve ranked prior applicants with relevant profiles.

matches.match_rationale: Building a local talent pipeline requires matching candidate profiles (availability, qualifications, location, specialization) against open roles across multiple campuses while satisfying certification and regulatory constraints. This is fundamentally a matching/assignment problem — the kind where constraint satisfaction excels — and it applies directly to the graduate trainee and alumni coaching programs the organization lacks.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A trainee placement engine that matches graduating teacher-college candidates or internal staff seeking upskilling to open roles and development slots across campuses, respecting credential requirements and location constraints.

matches.match_rationale: The organization lacks partnerships with nearby teacher training colleges and has no intelligence on local talent supply. A research agent can autonomously map teacher training programs in each campus's service area, identify relevant department contacts, compile program graduation timelines, and surface partnership precedents — giving HR a ready-made outreach list that currently doesn't exist. This directly attacks the 'limited partnerships with teacher training colleges' root cause.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A research agent that, given a campus location, produces a structured report of nearby teacher-training institutions, their specializations (STEM, special ed), graduate output volumes, and suggested partnership entry points.

matches.match_rationale: High recruitment costs are partly driven by reactive hiring — roles are posted only when they become vacant. Predicting which staff are at elevated attrition risk allows HR to pre-activate the local pipeline and referral programs before a role opens, reducing time-to-fill and cutting agency dependency for niche roles where reactive sourcing is most expensive.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An employee attrition propensity model trained on tenure, role type, campus, and engagement signals that flags at-risk staff 60-90 days before likely departure, triggering early pipeline activation.
